description: county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Tacoma ( tə-KOH-mə) is a city in and the county seat of Pierce County, Washington, United States.

description: county in California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno County ( ), officially the County of Fresno, is located in the central portion of the U.S. state of California.

description: national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: Japanese American concentration camp
Japanese American internment camp
description_wiki: The Jerome War Relocation Center was a Japanese American internment camp located in southeastern Arkansas, near the town of Jerome in the Arkansas Delta.

description: American concentration camp
description_wiki: The Heart Mountain War Relocation Center, named after nearby Heart Mountain and located midway between the northwest Wyoming towns of Cody and Powell, was one of ten concentration camps used for the internment of Japanese Americans evicted during World War II from their local communities (including their homes, businesses, and college residencies) in the West Coast Exclusion Zone by the executive order of President Franklin Roosevelt (after the bombing of Pearl Harbor in December 1941, upon the recommendation of Lieutenant General John L. DeWitt).

description: first national park in the world, located in Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho in the United States
description_wiki: Yellowstone National Park is a national park of the United States located in the northwest corner of the state of Wyoming, with small portions extending into Montana and Idaho.

description: river in Big Horn and Park counties in Wyoming, United States
description_wiki: The Shoshone River is a 100-mile (160 km) long river in northern Wyoming, United States.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

description: county seat of Missoula County, Montana, United States
description_wiki: Missoula (  miz-OO-lə) is a city in and the county seat of Missoula County, Montana, United States.

aliases: Royal City of the Holy Faith of St. Francis of Assisi
description: capital city of New Mexico, United States
description_wiki: Santa Fe ( SAN-tə FAY, -⁠ fay; Spanish: [santa ˈfe]; Spanish for 'Holy Faith') is the capital city of the U.S. state of New Mexico.

description: city in and county seat of Hidalgo County, New Mexico, United States
description_wiki: Lordsburg is a city in and the county seat of Hidalgo County, New Mexico, United States.

description: city in Napa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Napa is the largest city in and the county seat of Napa County and a principal city of Wine Country in Northern California, United States.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: sound along the northwestern coast of the U.S. state of Washington
description_wiki: Puget Sound ( PEW-jit; Lushootseed: x̌ʷəlč IPA: [ˈχʷəlt͡ʃ] WHULCH) is a complex estuarine system of interconnected marine waterways and basins located on the northwest coast of the U.S. state of Washington.

description: retailer in the United States
description_wiki: Montgomery Ward is the name of two U.S. retail corporations, one of which is defunct.

description: consolidated city-county and capital of Colorado, United States
description_wiki: Denver  (  DEN-vər) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Colorado.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Nebraska (  nə-BRASS-kə) is a triple-landlocked state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: large canal in Panama
description_wiki: The Panama Canal (Spanish: Canal de Panamá) is an artificial 82-kilometer (51-mile) waterway in Panama that connects the Caribbean Sea with the Pacific Ocean.
